expectations.text: States there is no universal cap - depends on goals, recovery, and time
expectations.evidence: States isolation is 'supportive, not primary' and asks 'how much isolation do I need to support compound progress without creating recovery debt?'

expectations.text: Notes that many muscles are only partially covered by compounds
expectations.evidence: For each muscle group, explains what compound coverage they get and what isolation addresses.

expectations.text: Provides a concrete baseline recommendation (roughly 2-4 challenging sets once or twice per week per muscle group)
expectations.evidence: Gives specific per-muscle recommendations: forearms 2-4, calves 2-3, delts 4-8, biceps 3-6, core 2-4 sets/week.

expectations.text: Warns that global/systemic recovery can become limiting
expectations.evidence: Includes section on 'When to Reduce Isolation Volume' noting recovery is poor, fatigue accumulates.

expectations.text: Recommends starting conservative and scaling up based on outcomes
expectations.evidence: Includes scaling by training age: beginner 6-10 sets, intermediate 12-20, advanced 20-30.
